title: malpighiodes bracteosa
intro: Malpighiodes bracteosa, also known as the False Malabar Kino, is a plant species that belongs to the Malpighiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Malpighiodes bracteosa include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
